binaries.title: ci.dll (Windows Code Integrity) - No security-relevant change (WIL feature-staging, staged catalog-policy enforcement, signature-database parser refactor)

changed_functions.name: wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState (sub_180019190)
key_changes: Function at exact address 0x180019190 is wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState (Windows Implementation Library feature-staging evaluation); patched counterpart is the same symbol at 0x1800192B8

Unpatched version walks a WIL feature dependency list at arg1+0x20, re-evaluating each cached feature entry via wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (sub_18001901c); bytes 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f are WIL feature-property/state bytes, not certificate fields
Patched version adds a (state & 0xc00) != 0xc00 feature-flag gate and calls wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (sub_180018fcc)
This is a feature-flag evaluation change, not an Authenticode signature-verification path
changed_functions.security_notes: The function at exact address 0x180019190 is wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState, part of the WIL feature-staging framework. It queries staging config via wil_RtlStagingConfig_QueryFeatureState (sub_18005448c) and walks a WIL feature dependency list at arg1+0x20, re-evaluating cached entries through wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (sub_18001901c). The 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f bytes are WIL feature-property/state bytes, not parsed certificate flags. The patched same-symbol function at 0x1800192B8 adds a (state & 0xc00) != 0xc00 gate and a wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (sub_180018fcc) call. This is not a signature-verification path and carries no signature-bypass primitive.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields (sub_180002D0C)
key_changes: Function at exact address 0x180002D0C is SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields, a statically-linked SymCrypt routine computing RSA CRT private-key parameters; it opens with call SymCryptFdefSizeofIntFromDigits
The same function exists in the patched binary at 0x18000E1E4 (same symbol); the apparent diff is a build relocation plus register-allocation churn
No arg7 bitmask, no i != 2 signature-count check, and no dual-signature logic are present

Helpers cited as signature comparison are SymCrypt big-integer primitives: sub_1800256dc = SymCryptFdefIntMulMixedSize, sub_180025418 = SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual, sub_180025934 = SymCryptFdefRawSubUint32, sub_180028404/sub_180025354 = SymCryptFdefIntCopyMixedSize
changed_functions.security_notes: The function at exact address 0x180002D0C is SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields, a statically-linked SymCrypt RSA CRT private-field computation operating entirely on big-integer objects. The same routine is present in the patched binary at 0x18000E1E4; the reported diff is a relocation with register-allocation differences, not a semantic verification change. There is no dual-signature cross-validation here and no signature-bypass primitive.

changed_functions.name: CiEvaluatePolicyInfoForCatalog (0x1800E0384 -> 0x1800E4C58)
key_changes: Function at exact address 0x1800E0384 is CiEvaluatePolicyInfoForCatalog; patched counterpart is the same symbol at 0x1800E4C58
Patched adds a new branch (0x1800E4D9D-0x1800E4E4D) gated behind Feature_TrustedLaunchSysAppsOSCatalog__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline AND *(rsi+0xa98) & 0x40000

That branch derives a package family name from the catalog full name at rsi+0xc18 via KappxDerivePackageFamilyNameFromFullName, allocates a buffer with ExAllocatePool2, and compares against the string at rdi+0x28 with RtlEqualUnicodeString, returning STATUS_INVALID_IMAGE_HASH (0xC0000428) on mismatch or when *(rdi+0x30) is NULL

When the feature gate is off or the 0x40000 flag is clear, control falls through to the pre-existing KappxValidateCatalogForTrustedLaunch path (0x1800E4E6C-0x1800E4E90), the same validation the unpatched build performs
The 0xC00/0xC08/0xBF8 -> 0xC20/0xC28/0xC18 offset moves appear in that retained old path and reflect a validation-context structure that grew by 0x20 bytes between builds
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a staged rollout of a new enforcement path for the TrustedLaunch system-apps OS-catalog scenario, not the repair of a demonstrable bypass. The new package-family-name RtlEqualUnicodeString check runs only when Feature_TrustedLaunchSysAppsOSCatalog__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline is enabled AND *(rsi+0xa98) & 0x40000 is set; otherwise the pre-existing KappxValidateCatalogForTrustedLaunch validation is retained. The offset changes are a struct-layout move, not a stronger reference. No attacker-reachable path is shown in which the unpatched build validates a catalog it should reject, so this carries no policy-bypass primitive.

changed_functions.name: CipFindFileHash (sub_1800D9CDC)
key_changes: Patched version threads sub_1800196ec() (VBS/capability check) into the signature verification dispatch loop
New branching: when sub_180019b18() returns true, additional policy verification paths are enabled (policy string array at var_b8/var_c8/var_c0)
Patched version adds handling for *(arg1+0xc20) and *(arg1+0xc28) fields (new verification context fields)
KeStackAttachProcess/KeUnstackDetachProcess paths gated by sub_1800196ec() in patched version
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the main signature verification dispatcher. Changes are primarily about extending the verification framework with VBS-related policy enforcement rather than fixing a specific vulnerability.

changed_functions.name: SbpParseSignatureDatabase (0x18000BDDC) -> SbpParseSignatureDatabaseFromBuffer (0x18001E1DC)
key_changes: Unpatched: signature-database blob parsed inline by SbpParseSignatureDatabase @ 0x18000BDDC; patched: that function (relocated to 0x18000E0FC) is a thin wrapper calling a new helper SbpParseSignatureDatabaseFromBuffer @ 0x18001E1DC that holds the parse logic
The diff engine mispaired the new helper against the unpatched I_MapFileHashes (0x180088580) (true patched I_MapFileHashes is at 0x1800DB5F0), producing the low 0.1828 similarity
Patched adds a third entry category and computes the allocation size with RtlULongLongMult checked multiplies plus compare-with-addend guards (0x18001E269-0x18001E2DD) before SbeAlloc
Direction: the unpatched allocation (count1 + count2) * 0x10 + Size (0x18000BE49-0x18000BE54) uses 32-bit counts (r14d/r15d) and a 32-bit Size, so the 64-bit product cannot wrap

The per-entry bounds checks (cmp rax,0x1c; jb; entry_end = cur + *(cur+0x10); cmp entry_end,cur; jb; cmp entry_end,end; ja; cmp ecx,0x1c; jb) are present verbatim in BOTH builds (unpatched 0x18000BE93-0x18000BEAD, patched 0x18001E368-0x18001E38E)
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a refactor of the signature-database parser plus defense-in-depth arithmetic hardening, not a vulnerability fix. The parse logic moved from the inline SbpParseSignatureDatabase (0x18000BDDC) into a new helper SbpParseSignatureDatabaseFromBuffer (0x18001E1DC), which adds a third entry category and RtlULongLongMult checked multiplies. The unpatched size arithmetic uses 32-bit counts and a 32-bit length, so (count1 + count2) * 0x10 + Size cannot overflow a 64-bit total, and the per-entry bounds checks are identical in both builds. No undersized-allocation or out-of-bounds-write primitive is reachable in the unpatched parser. The 0.1828 similarity reflects the diff engine mispairing the new helper with I_MapFileHashes (0x180088580).

changed_functions.name: CipMinCryptToAuthRoot2 (sub_180054C04)
key_changes: Patched version adds new output parameter (arg5) for certificate verification status codes
New calls to sub_180018df8() gate EKU validation paths
Patched version distinguishes between different verification failure modes (status codes 0x1b, 4, etc.)
New sub_1800d2b90 (cert policy validation) replaces unpatched sub_1800cf570 with extended logic
changed_functions.security_notes: Certificate EKU (Extended Key Usage) validation function. Changes are about enhanced reporting and VBS-gated policy enforcement, not a direct vulnerability fix.

changed_functions.name: CipValidateEndpointSecPolicyFile (sub_180058470)
key_changes: Patched version gates the file-reading path (ZwOpenFile, ObReferenceObjectByHandle, ZwReadFile) behind sub_180018df8() check
New alternate path when sub_180018df8() is true: uses sub_1800599a0 instead of direct file I/O
Object cleanup (ObfDereferenceObject, ZwClose) also gated behind sub_180018df8()
Different parsing functions called: sub_1800755f4/sub_1800723fc/sub_18007249c (patched) vs sub_180071704/sub_18006e57c/sub_18006e61c (unpatched)
changed_functions.security_notes: PE signature file reading and parsing function. Changes are about VBS-aware file access path selection.

changed_functions.name: CipShouldRevalidateForPolicyChange (sub_1800675E4)
key_changes: Address validation function: checks if an address falls within the valid system/user range
Patched version uses data_180045368 instead of data_180043370 as the range boundary
Patched version simplifies the conditional logic for address range checking
changed_functions.security_notes: Helper function for address range validation. Changes are cosmetic - different global variable for the range boundary due to build differences.

security_findings.description: The function at exact address 0x180019190 is wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState, part of the WIL feature-staging framework. It queries staging config via wil_RtlStagingConfig_QueryFeatureState (sub_18005448c) and walks a WIL feature dependency list at arg1+0x20, re-evaluating each cached feature entry through wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (sub_18001901c). The bytes at 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f are WIL feature-property/state bytes, not parsed certificate fields, and arg1+0x20 is not an Authenticode certificate chain. The patched same-symbol function at 0x1800192B8 adds a (state & 0xc00) != 0xc00 feature-flag gate and calls wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (sub_180018fcc). This is a feature-flag evaluation change, not a signature-verification fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None. This function is WIL feature-enabled-state evaluation and does not process attacker-supplied Authenticode data; the 0x1d/0x1e/0x1f bytes are internal WIL feature-property/state bytes.
security_findings.primitive: None - not a signature-verification path and provides no bypass primitive
security_findings.assembly:
wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState (unpatched) - feature dependency walk:
mov rdi, qword [rdi+0x20]    ; Load feature dependency list
test rdi, rdi
je 0x1800192d8                ; Skip if none
; --- Per-entry dependency loop ---
cmp byte [rcx+0x1e], 0x0
jne 0x1800192b2               ; WIL feature-property byte 0x1e
test bl, 0x1
je 0x1800192c4                ; state mask bit 0 clear -> skip
cmp byte [rcx+0x1d], 0x0
jne 0x1800192b2               ; WIL feature-property byte 0x1d
xor eax, eax
cmp byte [rcx+0x1f], 0x0
je 0x1800192c4                ; WIL feature-property byte 0x1f
mov r9, qword [rcx]           ; dependency entry pointer
mov eax, dword [r9]           ; cached-state DWORD
mov dword [rsp+0x58], eax
test al, 0x2                  ; bit 1 of cached-state DWORD
je 0x18001928d                ; if clear, recompute
mov rcx, qword [rdi]          ; reuse cached state
test rcx, rcx
je 0x1800192d8
mov eax, 0x1                  ; enabled-state result for entry
jmp 0x1800192c6
; --- Recompute path ---
mov rdx, qword [rsp+0x58]
mov r8, rcx
mov rcx, r9
call 0x18001901c             ; wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState
mov rax, qword [rsp+0x58]
jmp 0x18001929d
; --- Patched (0x1800192B8) adds ---
; mov edx, 0xc00
; and eax, edx
; cmp eax, edx ; (state & 0xc00) != 0xc00 gate
; jne 0x180019371
; call sub_180018fcc ; wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled

breakpoints.why: Function entry. RCX = arg1, RDX = arg2 (result out). At arg1+0x20 is the WIL feature dependency list head.
Start of the feature dependency loop. RDI holds the current dependency entry pointer. RDI+0x1d/0x1e/0x1f are WIL feature-property/state bytes.
Cached feature-state recompute helper (sub_18001901c). Called when the cached-state DWORD bit 1 is clear.

watch: RDI at 0x18001925e = current WIL feature dependency entry pointer (list at *(RCX+0x20))
BL (low byte of RBX) = running WIL feature-state mask
[entry+0] cached-state DWORD: bit 1 (0x2) selects reuse of cached state vs recompute via sub_18001901c
key_offsets: 0x18001925e: Start of WIL feature dependency loop
0x1800192c9: end of per-entry processing, feature-state mask updated
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: None. This is WIL feature-staging evaluation with no attacker-supplied input; there is no PoC. To confirm the identity, break at 0x180019190 and observe the call to wil_RtlStagingConfig_QueryFeatureState (sub_18005448c).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: The unpatched and patched (0x1800192B8) versions differ by the (state & 0xc00) != 0xc00 feature-flag gate and the wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (sub_180018fcc) call. No signature-verification verdict is produced by this function.
debugging_aids.notes: The function at exact address 0x180019190 is wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState, part of the WIL feature-staging framework. arg1+0x20 is a WIL feature dependency list, not a certificate chain, and the 0x1d-0x1f bytes are WIL feature-property/state bytes. The patched same-symbol function at 0x1800192B8 adds the (state & 0xc00) gate and the wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled call.

security_findings.entry_point: SymCrypt RSA CRT private-field computation (statically linked)
security_findings.description: The function at exact address 0x180002D0C is SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields, a statically-linked SymCrypt routine that computes RSA private-key CRT parameters. It opens with call SymCryptFdefSizeofIntFromDigits and operates entirely on SymCrypt big-integer objects. The same function exists in the patched binary at 0x18000E1E4 (same symbol); the reported diff is a build relocation plus register-allocation churn, not a semantic verification change. There is no arg7 bitmask, no i != 2 signature-count check, and no dual-signature logic. The helpers cited as signature comparison are SymCrypt big-integer primitives: sub_1800256dc = SymCryptFdefIntMulMixedSize, sub_180025418 = SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual, sub_180025934 = SymCryptFdefRawSubUint32, and sub_180028404/sub_180025354 = SymCryptFdefIntCopyMixedSize.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: None. This is SymCrypt RSA big-integer math with no attacker-controlled signature input; it plays no role in the signature-verification verdict.
security_findings.primitive: None - relocated SymCrypt math, not a signature-verification path

breakpoints.why: Function entry (patched counterpart at 0x18000E1E4). Opens with call SymCryptFdefSizeofIntFromDigits; operates on SymCrypt big-integer objects.

watch: This is SymCrypt RSA CRT math; there are no signature-count fields. Offsets 0x180002D81/0x180002E3C/0x180002FB3/0x180002FC5 are internal branches of the big-integer computation.
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: None. Statically-linked SymCrypt math with no attacker-supplied input; there is no PoC.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Identical semantics across both builds; the difference is only the function's relocated address.
debugging_aids.notes: sub_180002D0C and its patched twin 0x18000E1E4 are the same SymCrypt routine, SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields. The helper functions are SymCrypt big-integer primitives, not signature comparisons.

summary: This is a patch for ci.dll (Windows Code Integrity) with no delivered security-relevant change. The four changed functions examined resolve as follows. (1) CiEvaluatePolicyInfoForCatalog (0x1800E0384 -> 0x1800E4C58) adds a new package-family-name RtlEqualUnicodeString check gated behind Feature_TrustedLaunchSysAppsOSCatalog__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline AND *(rsi+0xa98) & 0x40000; when that gate is off the pre-existing KappxValidateCatalogForTrustedLaunch path is retained, so this is a staged rollout of new enforcement, not a fix to a demonstrable bypass, and the 0xC00/0xC08/0xBF8 -> 0xC20/0xC28/0xC18 offset moves are a validation-context struct-layout change. (2) SbpParseSignatureDatabase (0x18000BDDC) was refactored so its buffer parsing moved into a new helper SbpParseSignatureDatabaseFromBuffer (0x18001E1DC), which adds a third entry category and RtlULongLongMult checked multiplies; the unpatched allocation (count1 + count2) * 0x10 + Size uses 32-bit counts and cannot wrap a 64-bit total, and the per-entry bounds checks are identical in both builds, so the added guards are defense-in-depth. (3) 0x180019190 is wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState (WIL feature-staging evaluation, patched at 0x1800192B8) and (4) 0x180002D0C is SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields (statically-linked SymCrypt RSA math relocated to 0x18000E1E4); neither is an Authenticode signature-verification path. The diff engine mispaired the unpatched I_MapFileHashes (0x180088580, true patched counterpart 0x1800DB5F0) with the new SbpParseSignatureDatabaseFromBuffer helper.
